back: A list of all the commands a CPU can process.

front: In what form are instruction set commands written?
Why does a CPU need an instruction set?
back: So it knows which operations it can perform.

front: What happens if a program uses an instruction not in the CPU’s instruction set?
Are all CPUs guaranteed to have the same instruction set?
back: No, different CPUs can have different instruction sets.

front: Why are instruction sets important for compatibility?
back: Programs must use instructions that the CPU understands.
